title: Fix self-improver execution rollback and pending listing
reason: The self-improver is currently unreliable because its execution function is truncated and routes call a missing pending() function. reason: Self-improver approval and execution endpoints are currently open, which is risky for monetization readiness and production reliability. Adding optional API key protection plus a health endpoint that reports output and proposal counts makes the system safer to operate and easier to commercialize.

def build_youtube_pack(topic: str, niche: str, tone: str, buyer: str, promise: str, bonus: str, notes: str) -> Dict:
    title = f"{topic.title()} for {buyer} | {promise or 'A Better System'}"
    script = f"""
TITLE:
{title}

THUMBNAIL:
Headline: STOP WASTING TIME ON {topic.upper()}
Subline: {promise or 'A cleaner way to move faster'}
Colors: Black, cyan, white

DESCRIPTION:
A premium long-form YouTube pack for {buyer.lower()} in {niche.lower()}.

STRUCTURE:
1. Hook
2. Why people stay stuck
3. The core shift
4. Framework
5. Mistakes
6. CTA

LONG-FORM SCRIPT:
If you're trying to improve at {topic} and it still feels messy, this video is built to simplify the path.
Most people stay stuck because they collect ideas instead of using a system.
The better move is clarity, repetition, and cleaner execution.

BONUSES:
- {bonus or 'Prompt pack'}
- spin-off shorts
- CTA ideas
- thumbnail variants

NOTES:
{notes or 'Keep it premium and direct.'}
""".strip()

    return {
        "status": "done",
        "mode": "youtube",
        "title": title,
        "script": script,
    }

    request_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    job_id = create_job(
        "product",
        {**payload.model_dump(), "request_id": request_id, "client_host": request.client.host if request.client else None},
        build_product_pack,
        topic=payload.topic,
        niche=payload.niche,
        tone=payload.tone,
        buyer=payload.buyer,
        promise=payload.promise,
        bonus=payload.bonus,
        notes=payload.notes,
        base_url=get_base_url(),
    )
    return {"job_id": job_id, "status": "queued", "request_id": request_id, "mode": "product"}

def build_youtube_pack(topic: str, niche: str, tone: str, buyer: str, promise: str, bonus: str, notes: str) -> Dict:
    title = f"{topic.title()} for {buyer} | {promise or 'A Better System'}"
    script = f"""
TITLE:
{title}

THUMBNAIL:
Headline: STOP WASTING TIME ON {topic.upper()}
Subline: {promise or 'A cleaner way to move faster'}
Colors: Black, cyan, white

DESCRIPTION:
A premium long-form YouTube pack for {buyer.lower()} in {niche.lower()}.

POSITIONING:
Primary audience: {buyer}
Core promise: {promise or f'Get better results with {topic}'}
Tone: {tone}
Suggested offer: low-ticket digital product + upsell bundle

STRUCTURE:
1. Hook
2. Why people stay stuck
3. The core shift
4. Framework
5. Mistakes
6. CTA

HOOK OPTIONS:
- Most people are making {topic} harder than it needs to be.
- If you have been stuck trying to win with {topic}, this is the reset.
- Here is the cleaner system for {buyer.lower()} who want real momentum.

LONG-FORM SCRIPT:
If you're trying to improve at {topic} and it still feels messy, this video is built to simplify the path.
Most people stay stuck because they collect ideas instead of using a system.
The better move is clarity, repetition, and cleaner execution.
Use one framework, track what works, and turn small wins into a repeatable process.
That is how beginners stop consuming and start compounding results.

CTA IDEAS:
- Download the companion execution pack.
- Grab the prompt bundle linked below.
- Use the checklist and templates to implement this faster.

BONUSES:
- {bonus or 'Prompt pack'}
- spin-off shorts
- CTA ideas
- thumbnail variants

NOTES:
{notes or 'Keep it premium and direct.'}
""".strip()

    return {
        "status": "done",
        "mode": "youtube",
        "title": title,
        "script": script,
    }

def build_youtube_pack(topic: str, niche: str, tone: str, buyer: str, promise: str, bonus: str, notes: str) -> Dict:
    title = f"{topic.title()} for {buyer} | {promise or 'A Better System'}"
    script = f"""
TITLE:
{title}

THUMBNAIL:
Headline: STOP WASTING TIME ON {topic.upper()}
Subline: {promise or 'A cleaner way to move faster'}
Colors: Black, cyan, white

DESCRIPTION:
A premium long-form YouTube pack for {buyer.lower()} in {niche.lower()}.

POSITIONING:
Primary audience: {buyer}
Core promise: {promise or f'Get better results with {topic}'}
Tone: {tone}
Suggested offer: low-ticket digital product + upsell bundle

STRUCTURE:
1. Hook
2. Why people stay stuck
3. The core shift
4. Framework
5. Mistakes
6. CTA

HOOK OPTIONS:
- Most people are making {topic} harder than it needs to be.
- If you have been stuck trying to win with {topic}, this is the reset.
- Here is the cleaner system for {buyer.lower()} who want real momentum.

LONG-FORM SCRIPT:
If you're trying to improve at {topic} and it still feels messy, this video is built to simplify the path.
Most people stay stuck because they collect ideas instead of using a system.
The better move is clarity, repetition, and cleaner execution.
Use one framework, track what works, and turn small wins into a repeatable process.
That is how beginners stop consuming and start compounding results.

CTA IDEAS:
- Download the companion execution pack.
- Grab the prompt bundle linked below.
- Use the checklist and templates to implement this faster.

BONUSES:
- {bonus or 'Prompt pack'}
- spin-off shorts
- CTA ideas
- thumbnail variants

NOTES:
{notes or 'Keep it premium and direct.'}
""".strip()

    return {
        "status": "done",
        "mode": "youtube",
        "title": title,
        "script": script,
    }

Description CTA: {cta_text} → {offer_line}

BONUSES:

- spin-off shorts
- CTA ideas
- thumbnail variants

NOTES:
{notes or 'Keep it premium and direct.'}
""".strip()

        return value

@app.middleware("http")
async def attach_request_id(request: Request, call_next):
    request_id = request.headers.get("x-request-id") or str(uuid.uuid4())
    request.state.request_id = request_id
    response = await call_next(request)
    response.headers["x-request-id"] = request_id
    return response

    script = f"""
TITLE:
{title}

THUMBNAIL:
Headline: STOP WASTING TIME ON {topic.upper()}
Subline: {promise or 'A cleaner way to move faster'}
Colors: Black, cyan, white

4. Framework

@app.middleware("http")
async def attach_request_id(request: Request, call_next):
    request_id = request.headers.get("x-request-id") or str(uuid.uuid4())
    request.state.request_id = request_id
    response = await call_next(request)
    response.headers["x-request-id"] = request_id
    return response

title: Add stronger commercial outputs to YouTube pack responses
reason: The YouTube pack currently returns only a title and script. Adding thumbnail text, CTA copy, offer positioning, and monetization fields raises product output quality and makes the API more monetization-ready without major architectural changes.

steps.find:
def build_youtube_pack(topic: str, niche: str, tone: str, buyer: str, promise: str, bonus: str, notes: str) -> Dict:
    title = f"{topic.title()} for {buyer} | {promise or 'A Better System'}"
    script = f"""
TITLE:
{title}

THUMBNAIL:
Headline: STOP WASTING TIME ON {topic.upper()}
Subline: {promise or 'A cleaner way to move faster'}
Colors: Black, cyan, white

DESCRIPTION:
A premium long-form YouTube pack for {buyer.lower()} in {niche.lower()}.

POSITIONING:
Primary audience: {buyer}
Core promise: {promise or f'Get better results with {topic}'}
Tone: {tone}
Suggested offer: low-ticket digital product + upsell bundle

STRUCTURE:
1. Hook
2. Why people stay stuck
3. The core shift
4. Framework
5. Mistakes
6. CTA

HOOK OPTIONS:
- Most people are making {topic} harder than it needs to be.
- If you have been stuck trying to win with {topic}, this is the reset.
- Here is the cleaner system for {buyer.lower()} who want real momentum.

LONG-FORM SCRIPT:
If you're trying to improve at {topic} and it still feels messy, this video is built to simplify the path.
Most people stay stuck because they collect ideas instead of using a system.
The better move is clarity, repetition, and cleaner execution.
Use one framework, track what works, and turn small wins into a repeatable process.
That is how beginners stop consuming and start compounding results.

CTA IDEAS:
- Download the companion execution pack.
- Grab the prompt bundle linked below.
- Use the checklist and templates to implement this faster.

BONUSES:
- {bonus or 'Prompt pack'}
- spin-off shorts
- CTA ideas
- thumbnail variants

NOTES:
{notes or 'Keep it premium and direct.'}
""".strip()

    return {
        "status": "done",
        "mode": "youtube",
        "title": title,
        "script": script,
    }
steps.replace:
def build_youtube_pack(topic: str, niche: str, tone: str, buyer: str, promise: str, bonus: str, notes: str) -> Dict:
    title = f"{topic.title()} for {buyer} | {promise or 'A Better System'}"
    core_promise = promise or f"Get better results with {topic}"
    thumbnail_headline = f"STOP WASTING TIME ON {topic.upper()}"
    thumbnail_subline = promise or "A cleaner way to move faster"
    cta_ideas = [
        "Download the companion execution pack.",
        "Grab the prompt bundle linked below.",
        "Use the checklist and templates to implement this faster.",
    ]
    offer_stack = [
        "Core execution pack",
        bonus or "Prompt pack",
        "Checklist bundle",
        "Spin-off shorts ideas",
    ]
    description = (
        f"A premium long-form YouTube pack for {buyer.lower()} in {niche.lower()}. "
        f"Built around the promise: {core_promise}."
    )
    script = f"""
TITLE:
{title}

THUMBNAIL:
Headline: {thumbnail_headline}
Subline: {thumbnail_subline}
Colors: Black, cyan, white

DESCRIPTION:
{description}

POSITIONING:
Primary audience: {buyer}
Core promise: {core_promise}
Tone: {tone}
Suggested offer: low-ticket digital product + upsell bundle

STRUCTURE:
1. Hook
2. Why people stay stuck
3. The core shift
4. Framework
5. Mistakes
6. CTA

HOOK OPTIONS:
- Most people are making {topic} harder than it needs to be.
- If you have been stuck trying to win with {topic}, this is the reset.
- Here is the cleaner system for {buyer.lower()} who want real momentum.

LONG-FORM SCRIPT:
If you're trying to improve at {topic} and it still feels messy, this video is built to simplify the path.
Most people stay stuck because they collect ideas instead of using a system.
The better move is clarity, repetition, and cleaner execution.
Use one framework, track what works, and turn small wins into a repeatable process.
That is how beginners stop consuming and start compounding results.

CTA IDEAS:
- {cta_ideas[0]}
- {cta_ideas[1]}
- {cta_ideas[2]}

BONUSES:
- {bonus or 'Prompt pack'}
- spin-off shorts
- CTA ideas
- thumbnail variants

NOTES:
{notes or 'Keep it premium and direct.'}
""".strip()

    return {
        "status": "done",
        "mode": "youtube",
        "title": title,
        "description": description,
        "thumbnail": {
            "headline": thumbnail_headline,
            "subline": thumbnail_subline,
            "colors": ["black", "cyan", "white"],
        },
        "cta_ideas": cta_ideas,
        "offer_stack": offer_stack,
        "positioning": {
            "audience": buyer,
            "niche": niche,
            "tone": tone,
            "core_promise": core_promise,
        },
        "script": script,
    }

def build_youtube_pack(topic: str, niche: str, tone: str, buyer: str, promise: str, bonus: str, notes: str) -> Dict:
    title = f"{topic.title()} for {buyer} | {promise or 'A Better System'}"
    script = f"""
TITLE:
{title}

THUMBNAIL:
Headline: STOP WASTING TIME ON {topic.upper()}
Subline: {promise or 'A cleaner way to move faster'}
Colors: Black, cyan, white

DESCRIPTION:
A premium long-form YouTube pack for {buyer.lower()} in {niche.lower()}.

POSITIONING:
Primary audience: {buyer}
Core promise: {promise or f'Get better results with {topic}'}
Tone: {tone}
Suggested offer: low-ticket digital product + upsell bundle

STRUCTURE:
1. Hook
2. Why people stay stuck
3. The core shift
4. Framework
5. Mistakes
6. CTA

HOOK OPTIONS:
- Most people are making {topic} harder than it needs to be.
- If you have been stuck trying to win with {topic}, this is the reset.
- Here is the cleaner system for {buyer.lower()} who want real momentum.

LONG-FORM SCRIPT:
If you're trying to improve at {topic} and it still feels messy, this video is built to simplify the path.
Most people stay stuck because they collect ideas instead of using a system.
The better move is clarity, repetition, and cleaner execution.
Use one framework, track what works, and turn small wins into a repeatable process.
That is how beginners stop consuming and start compounding results.

CTA IDEAS:
- Download the companion execution pack.
- Grab the prompt bundle linked below.
- Use the checklist and templates to implement this faster.

BONUSES:
- {bonus or 'Prompt pack'}
- spin-off shorts
- CTA ideas
- thumbnail variants

NOTES:
{notes or 'Keep it premium and direct.'}
""".strip()

    return {
        "status": "done",
        "mode": "youtube",
        "title": title,
        "script": script,
    }

def _score_youtube_pack(title: str, script: str, promise: str, bonus: str) -> Dict:
    checks = {
        "has_strong_title": len(title.strip()) >= 20,
        "has_promise": bool((promise or "").strip()),
        "has_bonus": bool((bonus or "").strip()),
        "has_cta": "CTA IDEAS:" in script,
        "has_structure": "STRUCTURE:" in script,
        "has_thumbnail": "THUMBNAIL:" in script,
    }
    score = int(sum(1 for passed in checks.values() if passed) / len(checks) * 100)
    return {"score": score, "checks": checks}

steps.replace:
def propose(title, reason, steps):
    _validate_steps(steps)
    d = load()
    fingerprint = _fingerprint(title, steps)

    for existing in d:
        if existing.get("fingerprint") == fingerprint and existing.get("status") in {"pending", "approved", "applied"}:
            return existing

    p = {
        "id": new_id(),
        "title": title,
        "reason": reason,
        "steps": steps,
        "status": "pending",
        "fingerprint": fingerprint,
        "created_at": int(time.time())
    }
    d.append(p)
    save(d)
    return p

def _quality_score(topic, buyer, promise, bonus, notes):
    checks = {
        "has_topic": bool(_clean(topic)),
        "has_buyer": bool(_clean(buyer)),
        "has_promise": bool(_clean(promise)),
        "has_bonus": bool(_clean(bonus)),

def metrics():
    leads = []
    if LEADS_FILE.exists():
        try:
            loaded = json.loads(LEADS_FILE.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            if isinstance(loaded, list):
                leads = loaded
        except Exception:
            leads = []

    outputs = [p for p in OUTPUT_DIR.iterdir() if p.is_file()]
    by_source = {}
    consented = 0
    contactable = 0
    named = 0
    file_types = {}
    for output in outputs:
        suffix = output.suffix.lower() or "no_extension"
        file_types[suffix] = file_types.get(suffix, 0) + 1
    for item in leads:
        source = item.get("source", "unknown")
        by_source[source] = by_source.get(source, 0) + 1
        if item.get("marketing_consent"):
            consented += 1
        if item.get("customer_email"):
            contactable += 1
        if item.get("customer_name"):
            named += 1

    conversion_rate = round((len(leads) / len(outputs)) * 100, 2) if outputs else 0.0
    consent_rate = round((consented / len(leads)) * 100, 2) if leads else 0.0
    contact_rate = round((contactable / len(leads)) * 100, 2) if leads else 0.0
    named_rate = round((named / len(leads)) * 100, 2) if leads else 0.0

    latest_outputs = sorted(outputs, key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)[:10]
    return {
        "ok": True,
        "service": "Zerenthis Automation Core",
        "lead_count": len(leads),
        "output_count": len(outputs),
        "conversion_rate": conversion_rate,
        "consented_lead_count": consented,
        "contactable_lead_count": contactable,
        "named_lead_count": named,
        "consent_rate": consent_rate,
        "contact_rate": contact_rate,
        "named_rate": named_rate,
        "leads_by_source": by_source,
        "outputs_by_type": file_types,
        "latest_outputs": [p.name for p in latest_outputs],
        "public_base_url": get_base_url(),
    }

def propose(title, reason, steps):
    if not isinstance(title, str) or not title.strip():
        raise ValueError("title is required")
    if not isinstance(reason, str) or len(reason.strip()) < 10:
        raise ValueError("reason must be at least 10 characters")
    _validate_steps(steps)
    title = title.strip()
    reason = reason.strip()
    d = load()
    fingerprint = _fingerprint(title, steps)

    for existing in d:
        if existing.get("fingerprint") == fingerprint and existing.get("status") in {"pending", "approved", "applied"}:
            return existing

    p = {
        "id": new_id(),
        "title": title,
        "reason": reason,
        "steps": steps,
        "status": "pending",
        "fingerprint": fingerprint,
        "created_at": int(time.time())
    }
    d.append(p)
    save(d)
    return p

@app.get("/health")
def health():
    lead_items = []
    if LEADS_FILE.exists():
        try:
            loaded = json.loads(LEADS_FILE.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            if isinstance(loaded, list):
                lead_items = loaded
        except Exception:
            lead_items = []
    outputs = [p for p in OUTPUT_DIR.iterdir() if p.is_file()]
    latest_output = max(outputs, key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime).name if outputs else None
    pdf_count = sum(1 for p in outputs if p.suffix.lower() == ".pdf")
    video_count = sum(1 for p in outputs if p.suffix.lower() in {".mp4", ".mov", ".avi"})
    consented_leads = sum(1 for item in lead_items if item.get("marketing_consent") is True)
    return {
        "ok": True,
        "service": "Zerenthis Automation Core",
        "output_dir": str(OUTPUT_DIR),
        "output_count": len(outputs),
        "pdf_count": pdf_count,
        "video_count": video_count,
        "lead_count": len(lead_items),
        "consented_lead_count": consented_leads,
        "has_public_base_url": bool(os.getenv("PUBLIC_BASE_URL", "").strip()),
        "cors_origin_count": len(ALLOWED_ORIGINS) if ALLOWED_ORIGINS else 1,
        "latest_output": latest_output,
        "metrics_url": f"{get_base_url()}/api/metrics",
        "self_improver_url": f"{get_base_url()}/self/stats",

    }

def _monetization_stack(topic, buyer, promise, bonus):
    topic_tc = _title_case(_clean(topic, "Digital Product"))
    buyer_text = _clean(buyer, "Beginners")
    promise_text = _clean(promise, f"get better results with {topic_tc}")
    bonus_text = _clean(bonus, "Prompt pack")
    return {
        "front_end": [
            ["Core offer", f"{topic_tc} execution pack for {buyer_text.lower()} who want to {promise_text}."],
            ["Price band", "$19–$29"],
            ["Promise angle", f"Help buyers {promise_text} with less confusion and faster execution."],
        ],
        "order_bumps": [
            ["Bump 1", f"{topic_tc} prompt vault for faster implementation."],
            ["Bump 2", f"Checklist + template bundle tied to {topic_tc}."],
            ["Bump 3", f"Fast-action bonus: {bonus_text}."],
        ],
        "upsells": [
            ["Upsell 1", f"Premium bundle with prompts, templates, and examples for {topic_tc}."],

@app.get("/health")
def health():
    lead_items = []
